title: Logra
description: Gaming-first full-stack media-tracking app built around backlog decisions, progress, discovery, import/export, and reliable demo operations.

introduction.title: Logra
description: Logra started from a practical need: I wanted a product that matched how I actually choose what to play, manage backlog state, track progress, handle wishlist decisions, and follow upcoming releases.

Over time it grew into a gaming-first full-stack web app with a multi-scope shell, a local demo separated from real sessions, a public landing, admin tooling, data import/export, and operational flows designed to keep the public demo reliable while the product keeps evolving.

search_mechanics: The gaming catalog starts from controlled IGDB ingest, so Discover and Library work on a coherent dataset that stays filterable and optimized for frequent queries.
Search combines text lookup, workflow-oriented sorting, and featured ranking, so exploration surfaces not only correct results but the most useful ones for choosing what to play next.
CSV/XLSX import adds a second matching layer: preflight checks, candidate resolution, manual overrides, and platform normalization reduce mistakes when data comes from outside the app.
lessons_learned: A workflow-driven UI matters more than generic polish: it reduces friction and makes the product useful every day.
Keeping frontend, API, and shared contracts clearly separated is essential once auth, admin, import/export, and demo flows evolve in parallel.
Operations matter as much as features: seed flows, demo resets, release notes, and versioned deploys directly affect product credibility.
Guided tours, contextual feedback, and stable selectors improve not only UX but also debugging, QA, and future iteration.

react.description: React powers reusable cards, filters, detail panels, guided tours, landing previews, and settings surfaces across different product areas.

vite.description: Vite keeps frontend iteration fast while tuning dense UI states such as filters, edit mode, overlays, the public landing, and dashboard widgets.

react-router.description: React Router organizes scoped navigation, protected areas, the public landing, legal pages, and onboarding flows without duplicating the shell.

mui.description: MUI provides theme tokens and accessible primitives for a dense operational UI that still needs to stay readable.

nodejs.description: Node.js runs API and operational scripts for seed, reset, migration, catalog sync, and demo-fixture generation, keeping local and production workflows aligned.

auth0.description: Auth0 handles real-user authentication and sensitive role flows while the local demo remains separate from production-style sessions.

caddy.description: Caddy handles HTTPS reverse proxy and VPS publication, simplifying production routing between frontend and API services.

github.description: GitHub keeps feature evolution, release history, and operational changes traceable across a full-stack codebase.

github-actions.description: GitHub Actions automates deploys and controlled maintenance tasks such as demo resets, release notes, and data sync operations.

sentry.description: Sentry adds observability across web and API runtime flows, which helps catch real issues beyond local debugging.

pnpm.description: pnpm keeps the monorepo workspace coherent, especially when web, API, and shared contracts need filtered builds and repeatable scripts.

webstorm.description: WebStorm supports debugging and refactoring across web, API, shared contracts, and operational scripts.

codex.description: Codex/ChatGPT accelerates GUI iteration, scripts, and operational procedures while final architecture decisions remain manually reviewed.

typescript.description: TypeScript protects shared contracts across web, API, and packages, reducing payload drift during fast feature work.

fastify.description: Fastify structures modular API routes, session auth, validation, admin endpoints, and other operational surfaces with low overhead.

i18next.description: react-i18next centralizes IT/EN copy for the landing, onboarding, feedback, settings, legal pages, and dynamic product states.

mongodb.description: MongoDB stores users, sessions, gaming collections, feedback, announcement state, and operational metadata with migration/index flows for long-term maintenance.

igdb.description: IGDB supplies catalog metadata through controlled ingest, Discover fallback, and user-triggered materialization only when needed.

zod.description: Zod validates runtime contracts between frontend and API so settings, onboarding, import/export, and library payloads fail predictably.

tanstack-query.description: TanStack Query coordinates client caching, mutations, and invalidation so library, wishlist, admin, and recap updates stay responsive.

framer-motion.description: Framer Motion handles card, overlay, landing-preview, and detail transitions while preserving control in dense edit and guided-tour flows.

nivo.description: Nivo supports stats and admin analytics charts, turning library data into readable and more useful insight surfaces.

difficulties_faced: Keeping frontend, API, and shared contracts aligned while auth, admin, import/export, and demo flows were evolving at the same time.
Separating the local demo from real sessions so the public showcase stayed reliable without flattening the real product too much.
Handling CSV/XLSX import with matching, manual overrides, and platform normalization without turning the wizard into a fragile flow.
Balancing dense card information with a fast UX, especially around edit mode, filters, quick actions, and contextual guided tours.
Maintaining UX quality and operational stability across local environments, CI/CD, versioned deploys, and controlled maintenance flows.

v1.title: v1.0.0
content: Release the core product with demo login, game-library management, status/progress tracking, favorites, and multi-section navigation.
Introduce profile and appearance settings plus scope release gating so the product could evolve toward multiple domains without duplicating structure.
Build the operational base for seed, bootstrap, and shared demo lifecycle management.

v2.title: v2.0.0
content: Expand onboarding with multi-page guided tours and contextual card and stats tours to reduce adoption friction.
Deepen IGDB integration with ingest sync, data segmentation, Discover fallback, and clearer catalog-quality controls.
Strengthen security and operations with more aligned docs and more reliable release procedures.

v3.title: v3.0.0
content: Introduce Auth0 with real authentication, stronger roles, and a dedicated admin area for user management.
Add contextual feedback and traceable bug reporting so real UX issues flow back into maintenance work.
Expand stats and widgets while also refactoring the codebase structure to keep growth manageable.

v4.title: v4.0.0
content: Push Logra beyond the internal library surface with a public landing, legal pages, maintenance mode, announcements, and runtime observability.
Build a more serious import/export layer with CSV/XLSX wizard flows, draft resume, advanced matching, HLTB support, and better data-quality controls.
Make demo and operations more dependable through account deletion, dedicated fixtures, weekly recap flows, and harder environment safeguards.

v5.title: v5.x
content: Refine library import with drag and drop, row skipping, clearer warnings, inherited platforms, and more realistic QA fixtures.
Strengthen dashboard, stats, and Discover with better insights, platform filtering, performance indexing, and more useful local-demo summaries.
Clean up admin and maintenance flows with clearer access control, more manageable feedback handling, and further modularization of dense UI areas.
